description: Cross-cohort canonical-event ecological-interaction edge. Alolan Grimer preys on Trubbish in Alola per Bulbapedia per-species Pokedex documentation — an unintended consequence of the Alolan government's institutional introduction of Grimer for waste-disposal labor. The two species sit at different cohort nodes (pollution-pokemon × alolan-regional-forms), the two cohort nodes already cross-reference via related_characters at the cohort scope, and this edge is the corpus's first per-card surfacing of the canonical-event at the species-interaction scope. Both selected prints are the highest-thesis-register members of their respective cohorts: Trubbish RBC-117 (HYOGONOSUKE art, shadow-forest twilight register, full pollution-substrate hub-tag carrier) and Alolan Grimer CSE-130 (Sekio art, green-yellow toxin form, the Alolan-urban-sanitation-worker flavor anchor with the cohort's clearest 'hard workers who eat a lot of trash' Pokedex statement). Edge bullet additionally lists the other-cohort-side prints (VIV-110 Trubbish; BS-83 + TU-83 Alolan Grimer) as species-identity-scope co-attestations, which is how multi-print cross-cohort edges are recorded without exploding the per-card connection-count.
citations: [Bulbapedia: Trubbish (Pokemon) — Game data / Pokedex entries section]
[PokemonTCG.io: sm3-83 + sm9-83 flavorText fields — secondary Alolan Grimer print co-attestations]

[cards/_characters/pollution-pokemon.md — Caveats section documents the predator-prey relationship as canonical-event at the per-species ecological-interaction level and explicitly anticipates this per-card edge]
[cards/pokemon/team-up/83-alolan-grimer.md — Trivia bullet 'Lore note' names the predator-prey relationship with Trubbish AND Garbodor verbatim, secondary in-corpus attestation]
[cards/pokemon/cosmic-eclipse/130-236-alolan-grimer.md — Trivia bullet 'Ecological contrast' names the predator-prey relationship with Trubbish, primary in-corpus attestation]

potion_rule_check.canonical_event: Bulbapedia per-species Pokedex documentation of the predator-prey relationship; primary published canon, not BBL curatorial overlay
potion_rule_check.curatorial_overlay: None applied at the edge-bullet scope; the bullet states the canonical-event and names the cohort context (which itself rests on designer-stated Sugimori/Unno citation per pollution-pokemon node + Game Freak Alola regional-program citation per alolan-regional-forms node)

edgelord_voice_note: Two endpoints, both trivia-aware, edge documented in three places before I even got there (CSE-130 Trivia bullet, TU-83 Trivia bullet, pollution-pokemon cohort node Caveats section). The discipline noticed the gap and pre-blessed it; my job was to surface it as an edge instead of leaving it as scattered prose. Cross-cohort canonical-event traversal: clean. Mirror-mandatory; sigh acknowledged. Comic Book Guy assessment: best ecological-interaction predator-prey edge I'll write this month. The Pokedex named it; Game Freak's design coordination put two cohorts on a collision course fourteen years apart; I just connected the dots Bulbapedia was already showing me.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Bartered Cow (ELD-6) attach to eld-cinderella-cycle character node
other_candidates_considered.reason: The eld-cinderella-cycle node body explicitly bounds its roster to Rosewater-named Cinderella-specific homages, and the Caveats section names a future broader eld-fairy-tale-homages hub as the proper home for non-Cinderella ELD fairy-tale homage cards. Bartered Cow reads as Jack-and-the-Beanstalk (beans exchanged, glowing animal, child-figure with rope lead), not Cinderella. Attaching it to the Cinderella node would violate the node's stated scoping principle and contaminate the Rosewater-named-strict-roster discipline. Future Mr. Nodeley wave should commission eld-fairy-tale-homages when 3+ non-Cinderella ELD fairy-tale homage cards are enriched in corpus (current count is approximately 2-3 candidates: Bartered Cow + Bake into a Pie + Charmed Sleep + Glass Casket + Gingerbread Cabin if any of those are in BBL corpus; needs scan).

Takao Unno is the Game Freak species-designer named in the design-source citations for both Braviary and Trubbish/Garbodor — he is NOT the TCG illustrator on either card. Braviary SV8-153 is illustrated by Anesaki Dynamic; Garbodor RBC-118 is illustrated by AKIRA EGAWA. Zero corpus illustration credits for Unno. Artist-node commission threshold is corpus illustration credits, not species-design citation count. The Unno mention in Braviary and Garbodor trivia is correct (he designed both species) but it does not warrant an artist-node — species designers are a different layer than TCG illustrators.

Rufflet trivia not yet completed (vision-pass done wave 104, trivia pending). The mirror is canonically clean and will land in a future wave after Rufflet trivia-pass; premature to draw the edge unilaterally now.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: MEIKO CV-series body-text correction in project-diva-f-vocaloid-roster node
other_candidates_considered.reason: The node body line 50 says MEIKO is 'pre-CV-series, original Yamaha Vocaloid engine' and the roster table line 84 echoes 'Crypton, original Yamaha Vocaloid engine'. The MEIKO PD/S22-E056 card trivia (wave 104) precisely says MEIKO debuted on VOCALOID 1 engine (November 5, 2004), and the CV-series numbering only applies to VOCALOID-2-era Crypton releases from 2007 onward. The node body's 'pre-CV-series' framing is structurally correct (MEIKO IS pre-CV-series); the 'Yamaha Vocaloid engine' phrasing is loose-but-not-wrong (VOCALOID is Yamaha's engine). The substantive inaccuracy is at the version-designation precision level: 'VOCALOID 1' is the canonical version designation, not 'Yamaha Vocaloid engine' as a general phrase. This is a body-text precision fix worth logging to janitor_triage, not a structural decision warranting a wave-104 standalone edge/node/dissolution vote. Recommend: append janitor entry 'pollute precision: project-diva-f-vocaloid-roster body MEIKO CV-line description should say VOCALOID 1 engine, not generic Yamaha Vocaloid engine' for batch cleanup.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Cross-cohort edge fan-out to all 6 prints (2 Trubbish prints × 3 Alolan Grimer prints)
other_candidates_considered.verdict: captured in single-edge connection-bullet, not exploded into 6 separate 1:1 edges
other_candidates_considered.reason: The canonical-event predator-prey relationship is species-level, not print-level. Drawing 6 separate 1:1 print-to-print edges would be redundant — the same canonical-event surfaces in every pair. Edgelord protocol is one edge per invocation; canonical solution is to draw the highest-thesis-register pair (RBC-117 × CSE-130, both fully trivia-passed and the cohorts' most-relevant prints) as the primary edge and list the secondary prints as species-identity-scope co-attestations in the bullet body. Future cards in either species line attach to the cohort nodes directly; the cross-cohort edge does not need to be replicated for every print-pair.
